text.title: Go AppError: struct, sentinels, constructors, Code predicate
text.summary: Defines the AppError struct, sentinel variables, factory constructors, and an IsCode predicate that enables errors.As-based classification without pointer-identity fragility.

section.title: AppError struct

rule.statement: Define AppError with Code (string), Message (string), HTTPStatus (int), and Err (error). Implement Error() and Unwrap() to satisfy the error interface and support errors.Is/As chains.
rule.rationale: A plain error string carries no metadata. Typed errors let middleware read Code and HTTPStatus without a switch on message substrings, and Unwrap() keeps the chain intact for errors.Is predicates.

section.reference: Full apperror package with struct, sentinels, Wrap, and IsCode.

rule.statement: Declare package-level sentinel variables for each standard error class (ErrNotFound, ErrUnauthorized, ErrValidation). Provide factory functions (NewNotFound, Wrap, AsValidation, AsConflict) that return new pointer instances rather than reusing sentinel pointers.
rule.rationale: Reusing sentinel pointers allows callers to mutate the struct. Factory functions return fresh instances with per-request context (resource name, inner error) while keeping the sentinel as a comparison target via errors.Is.

example.note: Factory creates a fresh value; the sentinel is only used as a comparison in errors.Is if needed.

rule.statement: Add an IsCode(err error, code Code) bool helper using errors.As rather than pointer identity. This makes classification work even after JSON round-trips or struct copies.
rule.rationale: errors.Is on pointer sentinels breaks when the error is reconstructed (JSON deserialization, gRPC transport). errors.As on the struct type survives reconstruction.

antipattern.description: Using pointer equality: if err == apperror.ErrNotFound { ... }
antipattern.reason: Fails after fmt.Errorf("%w", err) wrapping, JSON deserialization, or struct copy. errors.As handles all cases.
